_stage: CPAL Stage 1 — Wrong-Model Identification + LP Level Classification
_special_case: PLATEAU FLAG TRIGGERED — second consecutive L2 session on this concept without progress
_grounded_in: Plateau rule: Chi et al. 1989/1994; Renkl 2002 — stalled learners need mechanism scaffolding, not more rule exposition

_concept_meta.java_concept: Type promotion, casting. Error: 5/2 produces 2 instead of 2.5.

_input_recap.student_message: I cast it to double already but I still got 2.0, not 2.5. I added the cast! Why is the cast not working? I wrote (double)(5/2) like the textbook example.
diagnostic_phrases_extracted: I cast it to double already but I still got 2.0
why is the cast not working

wrong_model_identification.wrong_model_description: (double)(5/2) produces 2.5 because it casts the result to double.
wrong_model_identification.wrong_model_origin: Cast = conversion of output, not operand
wrong_model_identification.matched_signal: I cast it to double but I still got 2.0 why
wrong_model_identification.match_evidence: All three documented ID-C signals appear: 'I cast it to double but I still got 2.0' (verbatim), 'I added the cast already' (verbatim), 'why is the cast not working' (verbatim). The student's own code reproduces the exact ID-C bug pattern: cast wrapped around the whole division, not the operand.

alternatives_considered.wrong_belief_short: Division always produces a real number (mathematical truth)
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: ID-A students are surprised that 5/2 isn't 2.5 at all. Daniel already knows it isn't and is trying to work around it — he's at a more advanced wrong model.

alternatives_considered.wrong_belief_short: Type is determined by the result, not the operands
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: ID-B students don't reach for casts. Daniel has reached for a cast — he's accepted that types matter, but misapplied where the cast goes.

lp_signals_detected.signal: Knows int/int = int and that a cast is needed
lp_signals_detected.evidence: 'I cast it to double already' — has the rule
lp_signals_detected.rubric_match: L2: 'Knows int/int = int in Java. Knows to cast.'

lp_signals_detected.evidence: '(double)(a / b)' — applies cast to result, missing that a/b already evaluated as int (= 2) before the cast can act
lp_signals_detected.rubric_match: L2: 'Cannot explain type resolution order or why the truncation happens.'

previous_session_summary.result: Daniel acknowledged the rule but produced the same cast-the-result error in homework

plateau_gate._what_this_means: Daniel has been at L2 for two consecutive sessions on integer_division without advancing. Continuing with rule-level exposition ('use casts on operands not results') is unlikely to help — the methodology cites Chi et al. 1989/1994 and Renkl 2002 showing stalled learners stop benefiting from more rule statements. The plateau gate forces a switch to mechanism-level scaffolding (trace_scaffold), where Daniel will hand-execute the expression token by token and watch the int truncation occur BEFORE the cast can act.
plateau_gate._overrides: Stage 2 (intervention selection) MUST honor plateau_intervention unconditionally. Even if RL agent's preferred action has higher Q-value, plateau_flag wins.

expert_benchmark_key_ideas: Java determines the type of every subexpression BEFORE evaluation, working from the inside out.
(double)(a / b): the inner a/b is an int operation (both operands int) → evaluates to 2 (int). Then (double) converts that int 2 to 2.0. The cast happens AFTER truncation.

To prevent truncation, the cast must change an operand's type BEFORE division: (double)a / b, or (double)(a) / b, or a / (double)b. Then division sees a double operand and uses double-division semantics.
General principle: casts apply to expressions, not to the future. They reinterpret a value that has already been computed under whatever rules applied at computation time.

downstream_effects.stage_2_intervention_filter: Plateau gate forces 'trace_scaffold' — all other interventions suppressed regardless of RL Q-values.

stage_3_prompt_sections.LP-1_current_understanding: Knows the rule (cast needed for double division), misapplied: casts the entire expression instead of an operand. Has been here for 2 sessions.
stage_3_prompt_sections.LP-2_wrong_model_context: ID-C (cast-as-output-conversion). LLM is told NOT to restate the rule — Daniel has heard it. Walk through the expression evaluation order in detail.
stage_3_prompt_sections.LP-3_target_understanding: By end of session: Daniel can produce three different correct casts ((double)a/b, a/(double)b, ((double)a)/b) and explain for each why placement matters; can predict the output of (double)(7/3) before running.
stage_3_prompt_sections._intervention_template: trace_scaffold expects a step-by-step expression-tree walk. The prompt builder will inject the parse tree and ask Daniel to fill in types and values at each node before reading the next line.
